film.title: Mohabbat
film.overview: Rohit and Gaurav become best friends. Both unknowingly fall in love with the same woman.

dialogues.dialogue: So you are my well-wisher, eh? That's right. And you desire me? Yes. Bloody lecher! Aren't you ashamed to be lying at this age! Your arrival has brought colour to my paintings, Shweta! Thanks, Mr Hussain. I came rushing. . .
dialogues.cause_raw: expressing sexual desire at an old age

dialogues.dialogue: You tell me that! Who are you? I'm a burglar! You appear to be respectable. . . Aren't you ashamed to steal?! Okay. . . but you haven't told me who you are! I'm the owner of this house.
Will you give them a cow for such a small issue? Why are staring at me? I told you to take it away. Shameless! Immoral ! Aren't ashamed? Don't you have respect for women? Teasing a decent girl?
Why are staring at me? I told you to take it away. Shameless! Immoral ! Aren't ashamed? Don't you have respect for women? Teasing a decent girl? You're trying to seduce me for a penny.
You're great in fighting with sticks. Who taught you? Master Tajuddin. Taju? He was my disciple. Have some shame, shameless! My disciple's disciple defeated you all. Son ! You look fit.
dialogues.cause_raw: defeated by a less experienced person

dialogues.dialogue: Come, son. Come, son. Come. Be ashamed ! He disgraced and humiliated the entire village. And you could not do a thing. They will summon the village council.
Go. I will take you to Shekhar. Come on. Aren't you ashamed to slap a woman? Now don't leave any stones unturned. You're about to kill your daughter. Now strangle us too.

Bless you, son. Come and sit down. For all your years in America, you haven't forgotten your culture! Why must I? I am proud of being an Indian. And it's our culture that is our strong point. Bravo! You seem to be a true lover of your motherland!
dialogues.cause_raw: maintaining Indian culture despite living abroad

dialogues.dialogue: . . my Rupa will marry Shekhar. My dear. - Father. Shakuntala, sometimes a man in his pride and ego gets so blind. . . .that he cannot differentiate between right and wrong. Your maternal love made me. .
